narrative: You spot something low to the ground near the base of a mossy oak...
interesting_fact: Wood Sorrel was historically known as Alleluia because it flowers around Easter.

questions.question: What plant is this?
What is the common name of this plant?
Does Wood Sorrel have any other common names?
options: Jack by the Hedge and Stinky Bob
It has no other common names

questions.question: What is the botanical name of Wood Sorrel?
Where is Wood Sorrel typically found?
options: Shaded woodland floors near mossy banks and tree roots
questions.correct: Shaded woodland floors near mossy banks and tree roots

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Wood Sorrel
options: Heart shaped leaves in groups of three
Leaves fold down at night or in bright sun
White flowers with lilac or pink veins
Thick waxy leaves with purple undersides
correct: Heart shaped leaves in groups of three
Leaves fold down at night or in bright sun
White flowers with lilac or pink veins

questions.question: What is Wood Sorrel used for?
options: Edible raw as a trail nibble, added to salads for a lemony flavour
Dried and used as a rope material
Applied to skin to treat rashes
Used as a natural fire tinder
questions.correct: Edible raw as a trail nibble, added to salads for a lemony flavour

questions.question: What does Wood Sorrel taste like?
options: Sharp and lemony due to oxalic acid
questions.correct: Sharp and lemony due to oxalic acid

questions.question: What potential risk does Wood Sorrel have?
options: High in oxalic acid — not suitable in large quantities
Can cause skin irritation on contact
Toxic to humans even in small amounts
No risks — completely safe in any quantity
questions.correct: High in oxalic acid — not suitable in large quantities

questions.question: Which species could Wood Sorrel be confused with?
When does Wood Sorrel flower?
What time of year is Wood Sorrel most commonly found?
Is Wood Sorrel native to the UK?
options: It was introduced in the 19th century

questions.question: What was Wood Sorrel historically used to treat? Select all that apply
How was Wood Sorrel applied medicinally? Select all that apply
options: Infused in water or boiled in milk as a drink
Made into a syrup from the juice
Applied as a poultice soaked in juice
Ground into a powder and swallowed dry
correct: Infused in water or boiled in milk as a drink
Made into a syrup from the juice
Applied as a poultice soaked in juice

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Wood Sorrel?
options: Biennial — takes two years to complete
Ephemeral — only lasts a few weeks

questions.question: What soil or habitat conditions does Wood Sorrel prefer?
options: Moist, shaded, acidic soils under tree canopy
Dry chalky soils in open sunlight
Sandy well drained soils on south facing slopes
questions.correct: Moist, shaded, acidic soils under tree canopy

questions.question: What plant family does Wood Sorrel belong to?
What is the origin of the name Oxalis?
options: From the Greek word oxys meaning sour or sharp
Named after the botanist Johann Oxal
From the Latin word for forest floor
From the Celtic word for three leaves
questions.correct: From the Greek word oxys meaning sour or sharp

questions.question: What is the meaning of acetosella in the botanical name?
